mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.64% provides room for further easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.3% is near historical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in real GDP growth and retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 741278M shows recent revision upward
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where both GDPC1 and RSAFS decline simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over a 2-month window signals supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 101.9493 shows recent revision downward from 102.551
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1400 thousand units annualized signal robust residential investment demand that supports economic growth and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1385.0 (revised from 1487.0) is near threshold
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of instances where HOUST > 1400, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment and declining unemployment rate signals strong labor market momentum that increases consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to move favorably simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 150k month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp over 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of instances where both PAYEMS and UNRATE move favorably, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 4%, it signals monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation, creating asset price pressures that produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days in this specific regime.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 330.293 and FEDFUNDS at 3.64%
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where inflation > 3.5% and FEDFUNDS < 4%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP growth above 2.5% annualized over two consecutive quarters signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate > 2.5% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 24055.749 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth > 2.5% for 2 consecutive quarters, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
